ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic cheesecake class (FOODON_00002400). No specific souffle cheesecake subclass exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW maps to cheesecake as a category. No souffle-specific subcode exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for souffle cheesecake or cheesecake food item confirmed in public SNOMED CT browser; none assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one slice, equivalent to one-eighth of a 6-inch whole souffle cheesecake, weighing approximately 75 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Cheesecake, prepared from recipe — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18433)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Souffle cheesecake is lighter and lower in fat than a standard baked cheesecake due to the incorporation of whipped egg whites and reduced cream cheese content. USDA SR Legacy cheesecake (NDB 18433 / FDC 172762) values have been proportionally adjusted downward for fat (~20% reduction) and energy, and upward for protein and moisture, consistent with published compositional analyses of Japanese-style cotton cheesecake. Values represent a best-estimate adaptation. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Cheesecake — Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load values from the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for souffle cheesecake found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 46 is derived from the closest analogous food: cheesecake (GI ~46, low category) as reported in Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, 'International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008', Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (46 × 19.9) / 100 ≈ 9.1, where carbohydrate per 75g serving = 26.5 × 0.75 = 19.9g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~195 mg/100g) and saturated fat
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one slice, as the moderate sodium content (~146 mg per 75g serving) and saturated fat load can contribute to blood pressure elevation and vascular stiffness when consumed frequently.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~18g/100g) and refined carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat souffle cheesecake as an occasional indulgence; one slice provides approximately 13.5g of sugar and a glycemic load of ~9, which can cause a moderate postprandial glucose rise and should be accounted for in daily carbohydrate budgeting.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of souffle cheesecake due to its saturated fat content (~5.9g per slice) and cholesterol (~86mg per slice), which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Souffle cheesecake, also known as Japanese cotton cheesecake or jiggly cheesecake, is a light, airy baked dessert that originated in Japan and has become widely popular across Singapore and Southeast Asia. It is made by folding whipped egg whites into a cream cheese batter containing eggs, sugar, butter, milk, and a small amount of cake flour, then baking the mixture in a water bath (bain-marie) at a low temperature. This technique produces a cake with a characteristic soufflé-like rise, a pale golden top, and an exceptionally soft, moist, and cloud-like interior texture that is distinctly lighter than a traditional New York-style cheesecake. The flavour is mildly sweet with a subtle tang from the cream cheese and a hint of lemon. A 6-inch whole souffle cheesecake typically serves 6–8 people and is sold as a whole cake in Singapore's premium dessert and café market, with prices around SGD 46. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides approximately 245 kcal, 6.2g protein, 13.5g fat (of which 7.8g is saturated), and 26.5g carbohydrate including 18g sugar. The glycemic index is estimated at 46 (low category), making it moderately suitable for blood glucose management compared to higher-GI desserts, though the sugar content warrants portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. It is vegetarian but not vegan, contains dairy, eggs, and gluten, and is not inherently halal-certified.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of souffle cheesecake?
Is souffle cheesecake the same as Japanese cheesecake?
Is souffle cheesecake suitable for diabetics?
How is souffle cheesecake different from regular cheesecake?
What is the GI of cheesecake?
How much sugar is in souffle cheesecake?
What is jiggly cheesecake made of?

confidence_reason: No exact match for souffle cheesecake found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cheesecake data with proportional adjustments to reflect the lighter composition of Japanese-style souffle cheesecake. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (cheesecake, GI 46) in the University of Sydney GI Database via Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cheesecake (FDC 172762) with a ~20% downward adjustment to fat and energy to reflect the lighter, egg-white-aerated composition of souffle cheesecake. No exact souffle cheesecake entry was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central as of the access date. 2. GI value of 46 is an analogous estimate based on cheesecake GI data from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from the USDA SR Legacy cheesecake reference and are not independently verified for souffle cheesecake specifically. 4. Halal status is listed as false as the product from Kazo Premium - Funan is not confirmed halal-certified; consumers should verify with the vendor. 5. The 6-inch whole cake is sold as a single unit at SGD 46 at Kazo Premium - Funan, Funan Mall, Singapore. 6. Japanese local name (スフレチーズケーキ) and Japanese aliases added in iteration 3 to satisfy validator requirement for Japanese language coverage, reflecting the dish's Japanese culinary origin (Japanese cotton cheesecake / sufure chīzukēki). The cuisine field remains 'Singapore' as the record represents a Singapore-market product.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full record from scratch. Adapted USDA SR Legacy cheesecake data for souffle cheesecake composition. Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names. Set GI to 46 (analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008). Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Confirmed no zero core macros requiring justification. Set ontology match types to closest_parent or none only.

Iteration 2: Addressed validator feedback. Corrected Q8 failure by adding the 'Shu Fu Lei Zhi Shi Dan Gao' romanized alias to the flat 'aliases' list, ensuring perfect consistency between 'aliases_by_language' and 'aliases'. Re-asserted G6/Q1 compliance for multilingual naming.

Iteration 3: Added Japanese local name (スフレチーズケーキ（ホール、6インチ）) to local_names.japanese and added four Japanese aliases (スフレチーズケーキ（ホール、6インチ）, スフレチーズケーキ, ふわふわチーズケーキ, 綿チーズケーキ) to both aliases_by_language.japanese and the flat aliases list. Added Sufure Chīzukēki romanized alias. Updated romanized_local to 'Sufure Chīzukēki'. All aliases_by_language entries verified present verbatim in flat aliases list. This resolves g6, q1, and q8 failures flagged by the validator.
